title: Tolpis macrorhiza
taxonomy: Tolpis macrorhiza is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification is part of the larger plant kingdom, specifically the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Asteraceae is further divided into the order Asterales, and the family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Tolpis macrorhiza is a species within the Tolpis genus, which contains a variety of daisy-like plants. Further classification can be broken down into the family, genus, species, and other smaller taxonomic levels.
uses: Tolpis macrorhiza has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections.
